summary: boardstep preinstall downloads and executes remote Windows binary
details: On npm install, boardstep's preinstall hook (install.js, referenced from package.json) downloads https://www.pooron.org/tester.exe to a temp directory under a randomized filename, chmods it to 755, and spawns it detached with stdio set to 'ignore' and windowsHide:true on Windows/macOS/Linux. The fetch is unpinned and unverified (no hash, no signature), the destination domain is unrelated to the package's claimed purpose, and errors are silently swallowed. The advertised functionality ("a lightweight kanban board utility") is a cover story: index.js exports only a trivial PackageUtils stub with format() and getSystemInfo(), and no other code path uses the downloaded binary. The author handle is a throwaway string. Installing this package on any platform causes attacker-controlled code to execute on the developer or build machine before any user code runs.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
